title: Tephroseris japonica
taxonomy: Tephroseris japonica is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification places it among the daisy and sunflower family. Within the family, it belongs to the subfamily Asteroideae and tribe Senecioneae. Further, it is placed in the genus Tephroseris and is a species of flowering plant. The specific classification of Tephroseris japonica is a part of its botanical description.
uses: Tephroseris japonica is used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues due to its supposed anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Its roots and leaves are often employed in herbal remedies. Some studies suggest that it may have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. However, more research is necessary to confirm these potential uses and their effectiveness in humans. Further investigation into the plant's medicinal properties is required.

intro: Tephroseris japonica, also known as Japanese rose, is a flowering plant species in the family Compositae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Tephroseris japonica include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Tephroseris japonica is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory issues.
